sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin Launches GPS III Satellite, Paving the Way for Next-Generation GPS IIIF Spacecraft
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin has launched GPS III Space Vehicle 10 (SV10), the final satellite in the GPS III series, which includes significant advancements like an optical crosslink demonstration and a digital Rubidium atomic clock to enhance resiliency and accuracy. This launch paves the way for the next-generation GPS IIIF satellites, for which Lockheed Martin is contracted to build 12 units, promising even stronger anti-jamming capabilities and improved services for both military and civilian users. The company is accelerating GPS satellite production using emerging technologies like augmented reality and digital twins, reinforcing its role in critical space-based infrastructure.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. Class C stock (US02079K1079): Is cloud growth strong enough to unlock new upside?
feed.summary: This article examines whether Alphabet's growth in cloud computing, driven by AI demand, can sufficiently offset maturing search advertising revenues to unlock new upside for its Class C stock. It details Alphabet's core business model, highlights Google Cloud and AI as key growth drivers, and synthesizes analyst views on the company's financial health and strategic direction. The piece also discusses competitive positioning, risks such as regulatory pressures and macroeconomic slowdowns, and the relevance for investors in the U.S. and English-speaking markets.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. stock (US92343V1044): Is its wireless dominance still the key to reliabl
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. is a major U.S. wireless carrier, focusing on 5G deployment and fiber expansion to capitalize on growing data demands. Its business model relies on predictable revenue from postpaid wireless plans and targets both consumers and businesses with high-speed connectivity. For investors, Verizon offers a defensive play with dividend appeal, though competitive pressures and high capital expenditures for network builds pose challenges.

feed.title: Carrier Global Corp. stock (US1442851009): Is its HVAC leadership strong enough to unlock new upside
feed.summary: Carrier Global Corp. is a leader in intelligent climate and energy solutions, focusing on sustainable HVAC with a business model that integrates design, manufacturing, and servicing across residential, commercial, and industrial applications. The company’s strategy emphasizes sustainability and digital transformation, leveraging R&D in energy-efficient products and strategic acquisitions. Carrier's strong market position, diversified portfolio, and commitment to innovation, particularly in heat pumps and chillers, position it for continued growth in the face of global decarbonization efforts and increasing demands for energy efficiency.

feed.title: Applied Materials and Advantest Form Innovation Partnership for Epic Platform in Silicon Valley
feed.summary: Applied Materials and Advantest Corporation have partnered to create an innovation center within Applied Materials' EPIC platform in Silicon Valley. This collaboration aims to enhance the integration of front-end manufacturing and back-end testing processes for semiconductors and 3D advanced packaging technologies. The partnership seeks to accelerate the development and commercialization of next-generation chips, particularly for AI and high-performance computing, by combining their respective expertise in materials engineering and semiconductor testing.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley expects positive market reaction for UK power stocks following government reforms
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley anticipates a positive market reaction for UK power generation stocks, including SSE and RWE, following government electricity pricing reforms that will increase the Electricity Generator Levy windfall tax. Despite the tax increase from 45% to 55% starting mid-2026, the bank estimates minimal earnings per share changes for affected stocks due to hedging and forward curves. Morgan Stanley recommends buying SSE and RWE, also noting the removal of the Carbon Price Support from April 2028 and a planned 2027 auction for asset owners to switch to Contract for Difference agreements.

feed.title: HQLAX secures strategic investments from Broadridge and Digital Asset
feed.summary: HQLAX, a digital collateral mobility solutions provider, has secured strategic minority investments from Broadridge Financial Solutions and Digital Asset in its Series C-1 funding round. This investment will fuel HQLAX's growth, including technology platform evolution, collaboration with Broadridge's DLR platform, and migration to the Canton Network, aiming to enhance collateral mobility in securities finance. Representatives from Broadridge and Digital Asset will join the HQLAX Board, pending regulatory approval.

feed.title: Advantest to join Applied Materials’ EPIC platform in California By Investing.com
feed.summary: Advantest Corporation is partnering with Applied Materials to join its EPIC platform in Sunnyvale, California, becoming the first automated test equipment company to do so. This collaboration will connect Advantest's testing capabilities with Applied Materials' front-end manufacturing technologies and aims to develop integrated solutions for next-generation semiconductor testing, particularly for high-performance computing. The move follows Advantest's strong financial performance, driven by demand for AI-related testing equipment, and recent upgrades from analysts.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley upgrades Arrowhead Pharma stock rating on drug trial outlook

feed.title: Corinth Pipeworks & ArcelorMittal integrate low-carbon steel for energy pipes
feed.summary: Corinth Pipeworks and ArcelorMittal are collaborating to integrate ArcelorMittal's XCarb® recycled and renewably produced steel into energy pipe manufacturing. This initiative, announced at the Tube trade fair, aims to provide low-emission infrastructure solutions for both conventional energy and hydrogen transport projects. The XCarb® material is produced using electric arc furnaces, significantly reducing CO2 emissions and helping customers achieve decarbonization and circular economy goals.

feed.title: Moleculin Biotech stock (US60855L2034): Is its oncology pipeline strong enough to drive investor returns?
feed.summary: Moleculin Biotech (NASDAQ: MBRX) is a clinical-stage pharmaceutical company focused on developing novel small-molecule cancer therapies, particularly targeting acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and solid tumors. The article examines whether its oncology pipeline, featuring lead product Annamycin and other candidates like WP1066, is robust enough to attract investor interest and deliver returns. It highlights the company's asset-light business model, focus on unmet medical needs, and the speculative nature of biotech investments driven by binary trial readouts.

feed.title: Carlsberg Dumps Coca-Cola, Bets on Pepsi in Nordic Markets
feed.summary: Carlsberg is set to make a significant change in its soft drinks strategy, replacing Coca-Cola with Pepsi as its bottler in Denmark, Finland, and the Baltic states starting January 1, 2029. This move will also see Carlsberg taking over the production, sales, and distribution of PepsiCo products in these regions, marking an end to its current agreements with Coca-Cola by the end of 2028. The shift is expected to considerably reshape the beverage market across much of Northern Europe.

feed.title: SAP to Lead Supply Chain Innovation at Gartner 2026
feed.summary: SAP will showcase its leadership in supply chain innovation, particularly in AI-driven strategies, at the 2026 Gartner Supply Chain Symposium/Xpo in Orlando. The company will demonstrate how Business AI and data-driven research can transform supply chain orchestration into a competitive advantage through various sessions and an immersive engagement zone. Attendees will have opportunities to network with experts and explore solutions for digital transformation.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Blackstone (BX) After The Recent Share Price Rebound?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Blackstone (BX) is currently overvalued, using both an Excess Returns model and Price vs. Earnings (P/E) analysis. While the stock has seen recent rebounds, both valuation approaches suggest it is overvalued, with the Excess Returns model indicating a 16.8% overvaluation and the P/E ratio exceeding its Fair Ratio. The article also presents opposing "Bull" and "Bear" narratives for Blackstone's valuation to help investors consider different assumptions.

feed.title: Could MakeMyTrip’s (MMYT) IDR Listing Plan Quietly Redefine Its India-Focused Growth Strategy?
feed.summary: MakeMyTrip is considering listing its Indian arm through Indian Depository Receipts (IDRs) instead of a traditional IPO, partly to manage tax obligations during secondary share sales from its Mauritius-based parent company. This strategy could provide broader access to Indian investors and use India-listed equity to fund future growth. The IDR listing, following an internal restructuring including the merger of RedBus India, aims to solidify its India platform and fund product investments, acquisitions, and marketing efforts, though it also increases exposure to India-specific travel demand risks.

feed.title: Caterpillar Inc. stock (US1491231015): Is its construction equipment dominance strong enough for new infrastructure upside?
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. is a dominant force in construction and mining equipment, positioned to benefit from global infrastructure spending and commodity cycles. The company's strategy focuses on expanding high-margin services, digital solutions, and sustainable technologies, appealing to investors seeking resilience and exposure to long-term growth trends. While strong leadership and a reliable dividend history offer stability, investors should monitor cyclical exposure, trade policies, and competition.

feed.title: Nucor extends HRC price increase streak
feed.summary: Nucor Corporation, the largest steel producer in the US, has increased its hot-rolled coil (HRC) consumer spot price by US$10 per short ton, reaching US$1,055. This marks the 13th consecutive week of price hikes, resulting in a 20.6% increase over the last six months. Prices at its California Steel Industries facility also rose to US$1,105 per short ton, with lead times for HRC delivery remaining consistent at three to five weeks.
